description_long: This house features a sprawling, single-story layout with a combination of hip and gable roofs, primarily covered in dark brown and gray shingles. The exterior walls are finished in a clean white, with several rectangular windows distributed along the facade, providing ample natural light. A distinct section of the roof is highlighted in purple, suggesting either a design accent or a different roofing material. The structure includes a main rectangular volume and a perpendicular wing, creating an L-shaped footprint. Visible gutters and downspouts indicate attention to drainage, while the overall design is simple and functional, typical of suburban or residential neighborhoods. The house appears to have multiple entry points, with the main entrance likely located at the intersection of the two wings.
description: A single-story, L-shaped house with hip and gable roofs in brown, gray, and a purple accent, featuring white exterior walls and multiple rectangular windows.
description_view_0: This view shows the long side of the house with brown and gray shingled roofs, white walls, and several windows; the purple roof accent is visible on the right wing.
description_view_1: From this angle, the purple-accented roof section is prominent, and the L-shaped layout is clear, with the main and side wings both visible.
description_view_2: This perspective highlights the gray hip roof and the perpendicular wing, with fewer windows visible and a focus on the rear and side of the house.
description_view_3: The view emphasizes the brown shingled roof and the back of the house, showing a large window or door and the intersection of the two main volumes.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entry likely at the intersection of the wings, with a simple door, minimal overhang, and no visible porch or decorative columns.
